questions.question: Which secure coding/web security concept best matches: development practice focused on preventing security vulnerabilities in code?
questions.explanation: Secure Coding is defined as: development practice focused on preventing security vulnerabilities in code.

questions.question: What is the most accurate description of 'Secure Coding'?
options: development practice focused on preventing security vulnerabilities in code
cookie attribute reducing cross-site request risk
unpredictable token validated to defend CSRF attacks
gaining higher permissions than originally granted
questions.explanation: Secure Coding refers to: development practice focused on preventing security vulnerabilities in code.

questions.question: 'Secure Coding' belongs mainly to which secure coding/web security area?
questions.explanation: Secure Coding is mainly part of Secure Development Fundamentals.

questions.question: Identify the correct secure coding term-definition mapping.
options: Vulnerability Management -> development practice focused on preventing security vulnerabilities in code
Secure Coding -> development practice focused on preventing security vulnerabilities in code

Secure Cookie -> development practice focused on preventing security vulnerabilities in code
questions.explanation: Only 'Secure Coding -> development practice focused on preventing security vulnerabilities in code' is correctly mapped.

questions.explanation: The requirement directly points to Secure Coding.
